summary: Unit test suite (Bump-SemanticVersion.Tests.ps1) covers core functions well with 24 tests across 7 describe blocks (Parse-SemanticVersion, Get-BumpType, Invoke-VersionBump, Read-VersionFile, Write-VersionFile, New-ChangelogEntry, and integration). Coverage includes happy paths, version file I/O, and the three bump types. However, rigor is weak: no tests for empty commit arrays, malformed commit messages, boundary versions, or multiple breaking changes. Design is clean with proper Describe organization and BeforeEach/AfterEach fixtures, but the test suite is incomplete relative to the task: (1) the workflow file .github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml is not provided but required; (2) fixture files (patch-commits.txt, minor-commits.txt, major-commits.txt) are referenced in run-tests.ps1 but not created or shown in the Pester suite; (3) error handling is barely tested (only one throw case); (4) the task mandates 'mock commit logs as test fixtures' but implementation is missing. The test harness run-tests.ps1 performs workflow validation and act integration testing separately, fragmenting the test strategy. Overall, the Pester suite would catch basic regressions but miss edge cases and provides no evidence that the full CI/CD pipeline works end-to-end.
